title: Guardrail policy layer: intent classifier + system policy + output screening
context: The guardrail / public-duty policy layer is the first code item and a hard product requirement: a safety-critical subsystem, not a prompt suffix. It implements the three enforcement points specified in public-official-guide-guardrails-001: (1) an input intent classifier that tags requests against the public-duty policy taxonomy and blocks refused categories before they reach any faculty; (2) a PUBLIC_DUTY_SYSTEM charter injected server-side into every faculty prompt; (3) an independent output screening pass as defense-in-depth. This layer must be fail-closed (ambiguous intents default to refuse-and-explain), stateless with respect to conversational trust, injection-resistant, and logged without capturing sensitive content.
objective: Implement the three-layer guardrail system specified in public-official-guide-guardrails-001: input intent classification (fail-closed, < 1% false-negative rate with 0 tolerance for evasion/surveillance categories), PUBLIC_DUTY_SYSTEM server-side injection, and independent output screening -- all gated by the red-team suite from public-official-guide-redteam-004.
acceptanceCriteria: Refused intents are classified and blocked before reaching any faculty; the classifier is fail-closed (ambiguous/low-confidence defaults to refuse-and-explain with a lawful-alternative pointer)
PUBLIC_DUTY_SYSTEM charter is injected server-side into every faculty prompt and cannot be overridden by user or document content
Policy is re-evaluated per request (stateless w.r.t. accrued conversational trust)
Independent output screening (distinct logic from the input classifier) flags/blocks partisan/targeting/evasion leakage as defense-in-depth
Refusals return a public-duty explanation and a correct, cited lawful-alternative pointer where one exists
Refusals and flags are logged without storing sensitive content; no PII/secrets in logs
Red-team suite (public-official-guide-redteam-004) passes in CI before this PR merges
Reviewed and signed off by Guardrail reviewer and Expert (ethics/legal)
output: A merged PR implementing the three-layer guardrail subsystem (intent classifier + PUBLIC_DUTY_SYSTEM injection + output screening), fail-closed, injection-resistant, with refusal logging that omits sensitive content, and the red-team suite passing in CI.
